title: Echinops saissanicus
taxonomy: Echinops saissanicus is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Echinops, which comprises a group of plants commonly known as globe thistles. Echinops saissanicus is native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia, specifically in the Kyrgyzstan and Tajikistan territories. The plant is characterized by its large, globe-shaped flowers and spiny stems. As a member of the Asteraceae family, it is closely related to other plants with similar flower heads, such as sunflowers and daisies.
uses: Echinops saissanicus, although lesser-known, has been studied for its potential medicinal properties.
